# Spec: <name>

## Metadata

- Status: `proposed` | `in-progress` | `blocked` | `approved` | `superseded`
- Created At: `YYYY-MM-DD`
- Last Updated: `YYYY-MM-DD`
- Owner: `<name or team>`

## Changelog

- Order entries from most recent to oldest.
- `YYYY-MM-DD` - `<author>` - <most recent change and why>
- `YYYY-MM-DD` - `<author>` - <older change and why>

## Related Feature

- Link the single feature this spec belongs to.
- If the note applies to many features, it should probably move to `architecture/` instead.

## Context

- Explain the technical problem or constraint this spec is addressing.
- Include enough background so future you can understand why this note exists.

## Current Plan

- Write the proposed technical behavior.
- Focus on data shapes, flow, validation, boundaries, edge cases, and tradeoffs.

## Data and Flow

- Describe what data enters this part of the feature, how it is transformed, and what comes out.
- Include references to architecture notes if shared systems are involved.

## Constraints and Edge Cases

- Capture limits, tricky scenarios, fallback behavior, or rules that must be preserved.
- Use this section to reduce ambiguity before implementation planning.

## Open Questions

- List decisions that are still unresolved.
- Remove items from here once they are settled in the spec.

## Related Implementation Plan

- Link `implementation-plan.md` once the coding approach is drafted.
